commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Claude CLI JSONL parser callbacks/types for tool-use, tool-result, and thinking deltas, wires tool events through the CLI lifecycle to channel preview callbacks, and expands parser/bridge tests.

Reproducibility: yes. from source for the parser gap: current main only surfaces assistant text deltas, while Anthropic documents `thinking_delta` events for streamed thinking. No live current-main or after-patch runtime reproduction was performed in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Human review should wait on contributor-supplied live behavior proof and maintainer stack sequencing; there is no narrow automation repair to queue.

target.title: feat(cli-output): emit thinking_delta events; handle redacted single-block shape
target.body:
## Summary

Mirrors the `onAssistantDelta` surface for thinking events on the claude-cli stream-json parser in `src/agents/cli-output.ts`. Adds an `onThinkingDelta` callback to `createCliJsonlStreamingParser` and a public `CliThinkingDelta` type that two downstream PRs depend on:

- #82285 (Telegram interleave) — needs the streaming-delta path to render thinking inline alongside assistant text in Telegram message previews.
- #81851 (cli-interactive backend) — needs both shapes to render extended-thinking and redacted-thinking output through the cli-interactive surface.

Stacked on #80046; merge after that lands. The diff here applies cleanly on top of the amend in #80046 (no overlap with the tool-event accumulator added there).

Two emission shapes are handled:

1. **Streaming-delta path** (extended-thinking on cli-interactive backend). `content_block_start` with `block.type === "thinking"` initialises a per-index accumulator. Each `content_block_delta` with `delta.type === "thinking_delta"` and a string `delta.thinking` field appends to the accumulator and emits `{text: cumulative, delta: chunk}` — same shape as `onAssistantDelta`.

2. **Single-block path** (redacted thinking on adaptive models). The API can return the thinking block fully-formed at `content_block_start` with no subsequent `thinking_delta` events. We detect this at `content_block_stop` time: if the block was a thinking type and no deltas were received and the start payload carried a `text` or `thinking` field, emit one event with that as the full content. This avoids silently dropping the redacted-thinking content on rendering paths. Encrypted-only blocks with no surfaced content stay silent (no spurious empty events).

Addresses [anagnorisis2peripeteia review 4345523435](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/80046#pullrequestreview-4345523435).

## Verification

Behavior addressed: claude-cli stream-json `thinking_delta` events were not surfaced from `createCliJsonlStreamingParser`; downstream consumers (Telegram interleave, cli-interactive) had no way to render extended-thinking content.

Real environment tested: tested via the parser's unit-test surface against captured stream-json shapes for both the streaming-delta path and the redacted single-block path. The parser is a pure transform; the unit-test harness exercises the full record-by-record dispatch identically to how `cli-runner/execute.ts` drives it on the live channel.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```
pnpm test src/agents/cli-output.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix:
```
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  28 passed (28)
```

The three new tests are:
- `emits onThinkingDelta for streaming thinking_delta chunks with accumulating text` — drives `content_block_start` (type=thinking) + 3 `content_block_delta` (type=thinking_delta) + `content_block_stop`, asserts 3 callbacks with cumulative text.
- `emits a single onThinkingDelta when a thinking block arrives fully-formed at content_block_start with no subsequent deltas` — exercises the redacted single-block path.
- `does not emit onThinkingDelta when a thinking block has no seed content and receives no deltas` — confirms encrypted-only blocks stay silent.

Observed result after fix: parser now emits `{text, delta}` events on both shapes; downstream PRs can subscribe and render. The 25 pre-existing tests in this file continue to pass unchanged (no regression to assistant-delta or tool-event surfaces).

What was not tested: live end-to-end against a real claude-cli `--include-partial-messages` stream that produces thinking blocks. The unit-test fixtures match the API shapes documented at <https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/extended-thinking>; downstream PRs (#82285, #81851) will exercise the live path during their own verification cycles.
